label: Passage Berthroom
description: Passage Berthroom is a narrow sleeping compartment cut directly off The Ironband Passage, its walls of weathered timber cinched with riveted iron strapping in the ship’s fortress-made manner. Two built-in bunks occupy most of the space, their frames bolted into the bulkhead above a footlocker recess and a peg-rail for gear. A shuttered lantern niche admits the corridor’s cold, veiled light, while canvas-and-leather baffles at the door seam limit the drift of ash-grit and metallic dust. Devotional cuts and warning glyphs are set into the threshold jamb, marking the room as sanctioned lodging within a siege-ward vessel.
